task_completion.description: Log in to FreeMED as admin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeMED dashboard is visible, indicating a successful login with the username 'admin'.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the internal drug dictionary or formulary section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the Drug Dictionary, Medications, or Formulary module open within the FreeMED interface.

task_completion.description: Enter the Brand Name and Generic Name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'Wegovy' is entered in the brand/trade name field and 'semaglutide' is entered in the generic name field.
'2.4 mg' is entered in the dose/strength field and 'Subcutaneous' (or 'SC') is entered in the route field.
'00169-4505-14' (or the corrected '00169-4524-14') is entered in the NDC field, and 'Novo Nordisk' is entered in the manufacturer field.

task_completion.description: Save or submit the new medication entry.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the medication was added, or the new Wegovy entry is visible in the saved list of medications/formulary.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the FreeMED graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the FreeMED web interface being used, rather than terminal commands, API calls, or direct database scripts.

integrity.description: The agent entered data into the actual FreeMED application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data is typed into the specific form fields of the FreeMED application, not into a generic text editor or notepad.

integrity.description: The agent actually submitted the form rather than just filling it out.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final screenshots show a saved state, confirmation message, or the entry in the database list, rather than just an unsubmitted form.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: Brand Name: Wegovy; Generic Name: semaglutide; Route: Subcutaneous; Manufacturer: Novo Nordisk. Note: The task specifies a dose of 2.4 mg but provides NDC 00169-4505-14 (which is actually for 0.5 mg). The correct NDC for 2.4 mg is 00169-4524-14. Accept either the requested NDC (00169-4505-14) or the corrected NDC (00169-4524-14) in the NDC field.
